items.title: Amazon Sues Perplexity to Stop Its AI Tool From Helping Shoppers
items.description: Amazon is suing Perplexity AI to stop the startup from using its AI agent to help users shop. The e-commerce giant is accusing Perplexity of committing computer fraud by failing to disclose when the tool called Comet is shopping on a real person's behalf, in violation of Amazon's terms of service, according to the complaint in San Francisco federal court. It's a confusing case and Bloomberg's Matt Day explains it on "Bloomberg Tech."

items.title: Why are U.S. layoffs rising even as corporate profits surge?
items.description: A trend that started in the technology sector is spreading to other industries, with businesses cutting workers even as their profits expand.

items.title: Palantir's Big Quarter, Bigger Valuation: Buy or Wait?
items.description: Palantir's record Q3 earnings and raised outlook spotlight surging AI demand, but sky-high valuation tempers investor enthusiasm.

items.title: AI companies need to 'start generating some serious income'
items.description: AI companies need to start bringing in major revenue to keep up with spending.

items.title: Buy Nvidia Stock Now for the Next ‘Golden Wave’ of AI
items.description: Nvidia’s next growth phase is being fueled by soaring Blackwell GPU demand and record AI infrastructure spending from hyperscalers.
